sourceMovie.title: Ju-on: The Grudge
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ju-on: The Grudge works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ju-on: The Grudge clicked for you, A Nightmare on Elm Street (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ju-on: The Grudge?
faq.answer: Try A Nightmare on Elm Street, Insidious: Chapter 2, Lights Out—each shares DNA with Ju-on: The Grudge while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ju-on: The Grudge?
faq.answer: Ju-on: The Grudge (2002) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ju-on: The Grudge part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ju-on Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
